idea-plugin.name: MCP Server for Code Intelligence
idea-plugin.description:
An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that runs inside the ReSharper/Rider backend process, exposing code intelligence features to AI assistants via HTTP.

Available tools:

  • find_usages — Find all references to a symbol
  • get_symbol_info — Get detailed symbol info: kind, type, params, docs, base types
  • find_implementations — Find implementations of interfaces/abstract classes
  • get_file_errors — Get compile errors and unresolved references
  • search_symbol — Search symbols by name across the solution
  • go_to_definition — Navigate to a symbol's declaration with source text
  • get_solution_structure — List projects, target frameworks, and references
  • browse_namespace — Browse namespace hierarchy
  • list_symbols_in_file — List all declarations in a file
  • list_solutions — List all open solutions across Rider instances

Supports C#, F#, VB, and any language with a ReSharper PSI implementation.

Multi-solution support: multiple Rider instances can run simultaneously — all accessible through a single MCP endpoint. Use the list_solutions tool to discover open solutions and target tools with the solutionName parameter.

The MCP server starts automatically on http://127.0.0.1:23741/ when you open a solution. Set the RESHARPER_MCP_PORT environment variable to use a different port.
idea-plugin.getting-started:
1. Configure your MCP client

Add to your MCP client config (e.g. Claude Code settings.json, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "resharper": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "http://127.0.0.1:23741/"
    }
  }
}

2. Open a solution

The MCP server starts automatically when you open a solution in Rider. No extra setup needed.

3. Use the tools

Your AI assistant can now call tools like find_usages, get_symbol_info, search_symbol, and more — powered by ReSharper's code analysis engine.

Multiple solutions

When multiple Rider instances are open, all solutions are accessible through the same http://127.0.0.1:23741/ endpoint. Use list_solutions to see what's available, and pass solutionName to target a specific solution.

Custom port

Set the RESHARPER_MCP_PORT environment variable before launching Rider to use a different port.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.3.1

  • Path-segment matching for solutionName: use a unique directory name (e.g. "my-repo") instead of the full path to target a solution
  • list_solutions now includes uniquePathSegment hints for solutions with duplicate names
  • Increase default snippet length from 200 to 2000 characters for richer context in tool results

0.3.0

  • Multi-solution support: multiple Rider instances accessible through a single MCP endpoint using primary/peer architecture
  • New list_solutions tool to discover all open solutions
  • Optional solutionName parameter on all tools for targeting a specific solution
  • Automatic stale peer cleanup when a Rider instance closes or crashes

0.2.1

  • Performance: search_symbol and symbol-by-name resolution now use R#'s symbol cache instead of brute-force PSI tree walking — orders of magnitude faster on large solutions
  • Fix cascading timeout bug: timed-out requests no longer block the R# thread, preventing snowball failures when parallel tool calls queue up
  • Increase tool execution timeout from 30s to 120s to handle queued parallel requests
  • Fix macOS plugin install path (plugins/ subdirectory)

0.2.0

  • find_usages: results grouped by project and file with counts; excludeDeclarationFile option; per-line deduplication
  • find_implementations: direct vs indirect classification with via chain
  • get_symbol_info: filter compiler-generated record members (operators, accessors, parameterless ctors)
  • search_symbol: dot-qualified queries (e.g. "IProfile.Fake") match by ContainingType.MemberName
  • list_symbols_in_file: filter get_/set_/add_/remove_ accessor methods
  • browse_namespace: flag generated source files

0.1.0

  • Initial release with 9 MCP tools
  • Language-agnostic PSI support (C#, F#, VB)
  • Symbol resolution by name or file position
  • Ambiguity detection with candidate list
